title: “One who has cut off craving, Without remainder, Dried up the swift stream — That monk abandons the near and far shore, Like a serpent…
content:
“One who has cut off craving,
Without remainder,
Dried up the swift stream —
That monk abandons the near and far shore,
Like a serpent sheds its worn-out skin.”

“One who has destroyed conceit,
Without remainder,
As a great flood destroys a fragile reed —
That monk abandons the near and far shore,
Like a serpent sheds its worn-out skin.”

title: - “Venerable Bharadvāja, what is the cause, what is the reason why these young monks, youthful, with black hair, endowed with the blessing of youth,…
content: - “Venerable Bharadvāja, what is the cause, what is the reason why these young monks, youthful, with black hair, endowed with the blessing of youth, in the prime of life, do not indulge in sensual pleasures and live the perfectly pure holy life for their whole life, completing it fully?” - “Great king, the Blessed One, the Knower, the Seer, the Arahant, the Perfectly Enlightened One, has said: ‘Come, monks, regarding those who are like mothers, establish a mother’s mind. Regarding those who are like sisters, establish a sister’s mind. Regarding those who are like daughters, establish a daughter’s mind.’ This, great king, is the cause, this is the reason why these young monks … live the holy life completely.”

title: “Monks, for any monk or nun whose greed is not abandoned, whose hatred is not abandoned, whose delusion is not abandoned — that one is…
content:
“Monks, for any monk or nun whose greed is not abandoned, whose hatred is not abandoned, whose delusion is not abandoned — that one is called one who has not crossed the ocean with its waves, its whirlpools, its sharks, its demons.

But, monks, for any monk or nun whose greed is abandoned, whose hatred is abandoned, whose delusion is abandoned — that one is called one who has crossed the ocean with its waves, its whirlpools, its sharks, its demons, who has crossed to the far shore, a brahmin standing on dry land.

One who has abandoned greed, hatred, and delusion
Has crossed this ocean with its sharks, demons,
Its terrifying waves — so very hard to cross —
He has passed beyond all bonds.

He has abandoned death,
Without attachments,
He has abandoned suffering,
With no further becoming.
Having reached the goal, he is immeasurable —
I say such a one
Has blinded the King of Death.”

title: Elder Sivaka: Impermanent are these houses, Built again and again. Seeking the house-builder, Birth is suffering. O house-builder, you are seen! From now on, you…
content:
Elder Sivaka:

Impermanent are these houses,
Built again and again.
Seeking the house-builder,
Birth is suffering.

O house-builder, you are seen!
From now on, you cannot
Build a house again.
All your rafters are broken,
The ridge-pole is shattered.
The mind has been pushed from the stream,
Here, it is destroyed.

title: Four wheels, nine doors, Full of defilement, tied by greed — Mired in mud, O great hero, What will be the future state Of such…
content:
Four wheels, nine doors,
Full of defilement, tied by greed —
Mired in mud,
O great hero,
What will be the future state
Of such a human birth?

(The Blessed One):
Having cut off delight and bondage,
Sensual desire and evil,
Having destroyed the root of craving —
Such will be his future state.

title: Standing to one side, the deity Subrahmā recited this verse to the Blessed One: This mind is always afraid, This mind is always agitated, What…
content:
Standing to one side, the deity Subrahmā recited this verse to the Blessed One:
This mind is always afraid,
This mind is always agitated,
What is wished does not arise,
What is not wished does arise.
If there is a way to be without fear,
Please tell me what I ask.

(The Blessed One):
Apart from enlightenment and austerity,
Apart from sense restraint,
Apart from abandoning all —
I see these things
That lead to safety
For all beings.

title: “What do you think, Māgandiya? Have you ever seen or heard of a king or a king’s minister who, while enjoying the five strands of…
content:
“What do you think, Māgandiya? Have you ever seen or heard of a king or a king’s minister who, while enjoying the five strands of sensual pleasure fully and completely, without abandoning craving for sensual pleasures, without removing the fever of sensual pleasures, has dwelt, dwells, or will dwell with craving cut off and mind inwardly peaceful?”
“No, Master Gotama.”

title: “That is so, great king. That is so, great king. All beings are subject to death, end in death, and do not escape death. Just…
content:
“That is so, great king. That is so, great king. All beings are subject to death, end in death, and do not escape death. Just as, great king, all the pots made by a potter, whether unfired or fired, are subject to breakage, end in breakage, and do not escape breakage — so too, great king, all beings are subject to death, end in death, and do not escape death.

All beings will die,
Life ends with death,
According to their kamma they go,
Reaping fruits of good and evil —
Evil kamma leads to hell,
Good kamma to heaven.
Therefore do good,
Accumulate for the future life,
Merits for the future life
Are a support for beings.”

title: “Householder, suppose there is a hungry, weak dog at a slaughterhouse. A skilled butcher or his apprentice throws it a bone that has been well-scraped,…
content: “Householder, suppose there is a hungry, weak dog at a slaughterhouse. A skilled butcher or his apprentice throws it a bone that has been well-scraped, thoroughly scraped, with no meat left, only smeared with blood. What do you think, householder? Would that dog, by gnawing on that bone — well-scraped, thoroughly scraped, with no meat, only smeared with blood — be able to dispel its hunger and weakness?” “No, venerable sir. Why is that? Because that bone is well-scraped, thoroughly scraped, with no meat, only smeared with blood; the dog would only get weary and distressed.” “So too, householder, a noble disciple reflects: ‘The Blessed One has said that sensual pleasures are like a bone — of much suffering, much despair, and the peril is even greater.’ Having seen this as it actually is with perfect wisdom, having abandoned the equanimity based on diversity, dependent on diversity, and having cultivated the equanimity based on unity, dependent on unity, where all clinging to the world’s materials is completely abandoned without remainder, he cultivates such equanimity.”

title: “Monks, form is burning; feeling is burning; perception is burning; formations are burning; consciousness is burning. Seeing thus, monks, a well-taught noble disciple becomes disenchanted…
content: “Monks, form is burning; feeling is burning; perception is burning; formations are burning; consciousness is burning. Seeing thus, monks, a well-taught noble disciple becomes disenchanted with form … with feeling … with perception … with formations … with consciousness. Being disenchanted, he becomes dispassionate. Through dispassion, he is liberated. In liberation, knowledge arises: ‘Liberated.’ He knows: ‘Birth is destroyed, the holy life has been lived, what had to be done has been done, there is no more of this state of being.’”
